mars.name: MARS — Miljøstyrelsens Administrative Registrerings System
mars.description: Official project registry for nitrogen reduction, wetland extraction, and afforestation projects under the Danish Green Tripartite Agreement.
mars.license: Public data (Danish public sector open data)

dawa.name: DAWA — Danmarks Adressers Web API
dawa.description: Official Danish address and administrative boundary data, including municipality boundaries.
dawa.maintainer: Klimadatastyrelsen (Danish Agency for Data Supply and Infrastructure)
dawa.license: Public data (Danish public sector open data)

natura2000.name: Natura 2000 — EU Habitat & Birds Directive Protected Areas
natura2000.description: Boundaries of Denmark's 250 Natura 2000 protected sites. Includes both terrestrial and marine areas. Marine/terrestrial split is estimated using name-based heuristics.
natura2000.disclaimer: Marine/terrestrial classification is heuristic (name-based with area threshold). For precise split, a spatial overlay with coastline data would be needed.

section3.name: §3-beskyttede naturtyper (Naturbeskyttelseslovens §3)
section3.description: All Danish §3-protected nature areas: heaths (hede), bogs (mose), meadows (eng), salt marshes (strandeng), dry grasslands (overdrev), lakes (søer), and streams (vandløb).
section3.disclaimer: §3 areas overlap significantly with Natura 2000 sites. Simple addition of the two overestimates total protected area. A spatial union (GIS overlay) is needed for the exact combined figure. EEA reports ~15% of Danish land as protected.

forest.name: Fredskov & Digitalt Skovkort
forest.description: Protected forest (fredskov) parcels and digital forest map 2022. Fredskov represents the legal baseline of protected forests. The 250,000 ha afforestation target measures *new* forest above this baseline.
forest.disclaimer: MARS tracks afforestation only within its water quality project system. Klimaskovfonden data is now fetched live from their WFS endpoint. The true national afforestation rate also includes state and private planting tracked by Naturstyrelsen and SGAV.

dst.name: Danmarks Statistik (Statistics Denmark)
dst.description: Official statistics on land area, forest cover, agricultural subsidies, and environmental funding.

klimaskovfonden.name: Klimaskovfonden (Den Danske Klimaskovfond)
klimaskovfonden.description: Voluntary afforestation and lowland projects tracked by the Danish Climate Forest Fund. Polygon geometries fetched via WFS; area computed from geometry.
klimaskovfonden.disclaimer: Area is computed from WFS polygon geometry (Shoelace formula). May differ slightly from Klimaskovfonden's own registry figures. The WFS layer may lag behind the Power BI registry.

naturstyrelsenSkov.name: Naturstyrelsen statslig skovrejsning
naturstyrelsenSkov.description: State afforestation projects managed by Naturstyrelsen. Project list from their website, cross-referenced against MiljøGIS WFS geodata for precise area measurements.
naturstyrelsenSkov.disclaimer: MARS has an 'NST Skovrejsning' subsidy scheme defined but with 0 projects registered. This data bridges that gap via WFS. Not all website-listed projects appear in the WFS yet.
naturstyrelsenSkov.marsSchemeNote: MARS scheme 'NST Skovrejsning' (5551b8e5-cc17-4e86-89a0-6e763f5594f0) exists but is empty. The ETL monitors it for future data.

established.description: Anlagt — projects actually constructed and operational

approved.description: Etableringstilsagn — approved for construction, not yet built

nitrogen.disclaimer: The 'total' figure includes all project phases. Only 'established' projects have been physically implemented. Preliminary and approved projects represent planned or in-progress reductions that may not materialize.

approved.description: Etableringstilsagn — approved for construction, not yet built

supplementary.disclaimer: Klimaskovfonden tracks 3 voluntary lowland projects (~30 ha) via WFS. These contribute to the 140,000 ha lowland extraction target, not the 250,000 ha afforestation target.

extraction.disclaimer: Extraction area includes all project phases. Only 'established' represents actual land-use change.

established.description: Anlagt — forest actually planted through MARS projects

approved.description: Etableringstilsagn — approved for planting, not yet planted

marsTotal.disclaimer: MARS only tracks afforestation within its project system. This represents a tiny fraction of national forest planting.

supplementary.disclaimer: Klimaskovfonden tracks voluntary planting (WFS). Naturstyrelsen tracks state afforestation (MiljøGIS WFS). MARS 'NST Skovrejsning' scheme exists but is empty — monitored by ETL.

baseline.disclaimer: Fredskov (578,950 ha) is the legal baseline of protected forests. The 250,000 ha target is *new* forest above this baseline by 2045.

marsNaturePotential.disclaimer: Nature potential areas identified in MARS project plans. These are potential restoration sites, not yet implemented.

nature.combinedEstimateDisclaimer: Natura 2000 (~18%) and §3 (~9.5%) overlap significantly (~30%). The combined 15.3% is an OECD reference estimate. A spatial GIS overlay of actual designation boundaries is needed for the exact figure.

sketches.description: Skitse — early concept, not yet formally assessed

projectPipeline.disclaimer: Project counts from MARS plan-level aggregation. Only 'established' projects represent actual environmental impact. The pipeline shows progression from concept to implementation.
